content.claim: During 1995–2003, the number of small rural Finnish primary schools with fewer than 50 pupils decreased dramatically, coinciding with municipal decentralization and the 1990s recession.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Finnish system reform lens
limitations: Temporal correlation established; causal attribution to decentralization versus recession requires further specification
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
